title: Iranecio paucilobus
taxonomy: Iranecio paucilobus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Iranecio, which is a group of plants native to the southwestern part of the Iberian Peninsula. Within the family Asteraceae, Iranecio paucilobus is further classified as a member of the tribe Senecioneae. The plant is closely related to other species in the genus Iranecio, such as Iranecio fulgens and Iranecio carvalhoi. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification and relationships of Iranecio paucilobus.

intro: Iranecio paucilobus, also known as Paucilobus, is a plant species within the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to certain regions of the world. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and other minor health issues.
